records.ety: [OE. gropen, gropien, grapien, AS. grāpian to touch, grope, fr. grīpan to gripe. See Gripe.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Groped (grōpt); p. pr. & vb. n. Groping.]

defs: To feel with or use the hands; to handle.

To search or attempt to find something in the dark, or, as a blind person, by feeling; to move about hesitatingly, as in darkness or obscurity; to feel one's way, as with the hands, when one can not see.
quotes: We grope for the wall like the blind.
To grope a little longer among the miseries and sensualities ot a worldly life.

defs: To search out by feeling in the dark; as, we groped our way at midnight.
To examine; to test; to sound.
quotes: Felix gropeth him, thinking to have a bribe.
